title: Exact q-weighted geometric series
statement: For B = qM + k with gcd(B, M) = 1 and 0 <= k < M, we have 1/M = q/(B-k) = Σ_{j>=0} qk^j / B^(j+1).
proof_status: elementary algebraic derivation in docs and code comments

title: Order of a power
statement: If r = ord_n(a), then ord_n(a^m) = r / gcd(r, m).
proof_status: used explicitly in Python and indirectly in Lean classification

title: Exact QR-generator stride classification
statement: For an odd prime p and h = (p-1)/2, the stride m is QR-generating for base B exactly when ord_p(B) / gcd(ord_p(B), m) = h. Therefore QR-generating strides exist iff ord_p(B) is h or 2h, and their count is φ(h).
repo_status: fully formalized in Lean at the base level and backed by Python counterexample/regression tests
proof_status: formalized in Lean as pow_isQRGenerator_iff, pow_isQRGenerator_iff_coprime_half, qrGenerator_pow_count_eq_totient, and the base-level full-order reduction theorems culminating in base_qrGenerator_pow_count_eq_totient

title: Composite period via CRT
statement: If M = Π p_i^{e_i} is coprime to the base B, then ord_M(B) is the least common multiple of the local orders ord_{p_i^{e_i}}(B).
repo_status: finite prime-power CRT order theorem formalized in Lean and implemented computationally in Python
proof_status: formalized in Lean via the finite-product order theorem orderOf_pi and the prime-power CRT theorem orderOf_unitsEquivPrimePowers; the pairwise theorem orderOf_unitsChineseRemainder remains as the two-factor core

title: Preperiod from stripped base factors
statement: For denominator N and base b = Π p_i^{f_i}, the preperiod length is max_i ceil(v_{p_i}(N)/f_i); stripping those base factors leaves the purely periodic modulus M.
repo_status: implemented, tested, and globally formalized in Lean at the base-prime support level
proof_status: formalized in Lean as the max-over-base-primes step count together with the combined base-supported divisibility theorem; the stripped-modulus M statement remains classical/computational

title: Carry as a deterministic windowed transducer
statement: For a finite coefficient word c_0, ..., c_{L-1}, carry propagation can be modeled by a deterministic transducer whose state is the incoming carry from less significant blocks.
repo_status: explicit Python model with state graphs, coarse minimization hooks, carry-vs-remainder comparison reports, and tests
proof_status: construction-level result with explicit implemented/open boundary reporting, not separately formalized in Lean or Agda

title: Small residue improves visual legibility
statement: Small k, and especially q = 1, tends to postpone visible carry interactions and make the early block skeleton easier to read.

title: Good coordinates require positive q
statement: A mode m only gives a usable q-weighted block coordinate when B = base^m exceeds the periodic modulus M, so q = (B-k)/M is positive.
proof_status: definition-level guard now enforced in mode search

title: Exact incoming-carry boundary from the geometric tail
statement: For a block coordinate B = qN + k with 0 < k < B, the incoming carry into block j of the raw coefficient stream qk^j equals floor(qk^(j+1)/(B-k)). Therefore the first incoming-carry position is the least j with qk^(j+1) >= B-k.
repo_status: implemented and test-backed in the visibility layer
proof_status: direct geometric-tail identity encoded and checked against canonical and swept examples

title: Same-core threshold shifts are controlled by q-ratio intervals
statement: Fix a block coordinate with shared B and k, and compare an actual denominator to its stripped periodic core. If k^s <= (q_core / q_actual) < k^(s+1), then the incoming-carry and local-overflow thresholds for the core occur earlier by either s or s+1 blocks. When q_core / q_actual = k^s exactly, the shift is exactly s blocks.
repo_status: implemented and tested for same-core comparison objects and canonical family examples
proof_status: direct threshold inequality encoded in the visibility family layer

title: Exact visibility threshold for carried prefixes
statement: There is a sharp arithmetic criterion, in terms of q, k, B, and the period data, for the minimal stabilization lookahead and the remaining global visibility behavior beyond the now-implemented incoming-carry and raw-prefix boundary formulas.
repo_status: tracked with exact observables, an implemented incoming-carry boundary formula, canonical case families, and exported visibility datasets; the remaining lookahead formula remains open

title: Canonical factorization of long division into orbit and carry
statement: The long-division DFA factors canonically into a remainder-orbit system together with a carry transducer for all coprime bases and moduli.
repo_status: tracked with explicit comparison interfaces, state-map candidate reports, bounded counterexample search, and an open-boundary statement separating state relabeling, quotient candidates, and the global theorem
